title: sorbus aria subsp. umbellata
intro: Sorbus aria subsp. umbellata, also known as the Whitebeam, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The Whitebeam is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of the Whitebeam can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
